featureName: BUG-099-001: smackerel_assert_host_resources_profile takes the host-native `go run ./cmd/preflight` path whenever host Go exists, but internal/preflight reads Linux /proc/meminfo (absent on macOS), so the spec-099 pre-flight crashes ("read host RAM: open /proc/meminfo: no such file or directory") on every macOS dev host, blocking BOTH the heavy `test integration` lane and the stores-only `test integration-light` lane (wsl-macos-compatibility violation)
certifiedBy: bubbles.devops (in-session real re-run evidence on a macOS host)
discoveredBy: bubbles.devops — running `./smackerel.sh --env test test integration-light` on a macOS host (OPS-005 F-RUNBOOK follow-on)
rootCause: smackerel_assert_host_resources_profile (smackerel.sh) selected the host-native `go run ./cmd/preflight` branch whenever `command -v go` succeeded. cmd/preflight -> internal/preflight.ReadMemAvailableMB() reads Linux /proc/meminfo, which does not exist on macOS/darwin, so the gate fails-loud ("read host RAM: open /proc/meminfo: no such file or directory") before producing a verdict. Because both lanes share this helper (heavy via smackerel_assert_host_resources -> _profile <env> heavy; light via _profile test light), the crash blocks ALL gated ops on macOS.
fix: Gate the host-native branch on Linux: `if [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Linux" ]] && command -v go >/dev/null 2>&1; then <host-native> else require_docker; run_go_tooling .../preflight.sh <env> <profile> fi`. On macOS (or Linux without host Go) the existing dockerized runner reads the Docker VM's /proc/meminfo (NO --memory cgroup limit) — the correct 'can I bring up containers' signal, since all containers share that VM's RAM. internal/preflight is intentionally NOT taught to read macOS sysctl/vm_stat (that would gate on the wrong macOS-host number). NO-DEFAULTS preserved: both args still required; cmd/preflight / preflight.sh still reject a missing --profile.
provenBy: Real in-session re-run on a macOS host (2026-06-30): './smackerel.sh --env test test integration-light --go-run TestCaptureDisconnectDurability_ProcessorSurvivesClientCancel' now reaches a REAL pre-flight verdict via the dockerized runner — 'Smackerel pre-flight resource check: OK / RAM available: 8024 MB (required >= 2000 MB) / Disk available: 3155880 MB / 3081.9 GB (required >= 8 GB)' — instead of the previous 'read host RAM: open /proc/meminfo: no such file or directory' crash. The /proc/meminfo crash is gone; the gate produces a real number. Evidence captured in this packet's bug.md.
downstreamFinding: The same re-run surfaced a SEPARATE macOS-compat bug (NOT the preflight): bare 'timeout' / 'timeout --kill-after' in the test-lane orchestrators (smackerel.sh integration / integration-light / e2e) and the runtime-health scripts has no gtimeout/watchdog fallback, so 'timeout: command not found' (exit 127) on macOS blocks the stores-up + durability run AFTER the preflight passes. Filed as BUG-099-002-macos-timeout-not-found (distinct root cause; tracked separately). The durability test TestCaptureDisconnectDurability_ProcessorSurvivesClientCancel therefore did NOT execute this session; BUG-069-002/003 fixSequence order-2 stays pending.
blockedOps: ./smackerel.sh build | up | test e2e | test e2e-ui | test stress | pre-flight (every heavy-gated op routes through the same helper)
affectedSurface: smackerel.sh :: smackerel_assert_host_resources_profile (host-native vs dockerized path selection)

fixSequence.task: Make smackerel_assert_host_resources_profile OS-aware: take the host-native `go run ./cmd/preflight` branch ONLY on Linux; on macOS route through the dockerized run_go_tooling preflight.sh path (reads the Docker VM /proc/meminfo). Update the function comment with the macOS=dockerized=Docker-VM-memory rationale. Keep NO-DEFAULTS. Validate shellcheck -x + bash -n on the touched shell. Confirm the heavy `test integration` lane shares the same helper so one fix repairs both lanes. PROVEN once `./smackerel.sh --env test test integration-light` reaches a real pre-flight verdict (real current-vs-required number, not a /proc/meminfo crash). Flip BUG-099-001 to done when proven.
